series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests by an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention
period.

series_description:
Complaints and other unsolicited feedback
received by an agency from the public
concerning the agency and records
pertaining to the resolution of complaints.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of item
number 1.1.048.

series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal
correspondence pertaining to the
formulation planning, implementation,
interpretation, modification, or
redefinition of the programs, services, or
projects of an agency and the
administrative regulation, policies, and
procedures that govern them.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: This records series and
item number 1.1.008 should be
used only for correspondence that
is not included in or directly related
to another records series on the
agency's approved schedule. For
example, a memorandum that
documents an appropriations
request must be retained for the
minimum retention period
prescribed by item number 1.1.004;
a letter concerning an audit for that
prescribed by item number 1.1.002,
etc. SEE ALSO item number 1.1.011.

series_description:
Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and
internal correspondence, in any media,
pertaining to or arising from the routine
operations of the policies, programs,
services, or projects of an agency.
comments:
SEE comment to item number
1.1.007. SEE ALSO item number
1.1.010.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records maintained by staff during
business hours that document
appointments, itineraries, and other
activities of agency officials or employees
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected officials,
agency heads, and board or
commission members require
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_description:
News, press releases, or any public
relations files maintained or issued by an
agency. Includes print, electronic, audio,
and audiovisual records.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under Public Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code). Also includes
withdrawn requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are excepted under the Public
Information Act (Chapter 552, Government
Code), including records provided to the
legislature under section 552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure
of an agency and the relationships and
relative ranks of its departments and job
positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are
not considered archival.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
not included in or directly related to other
records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Surveys soliciting feedback from
customers, clients, or stakeholders of an
agency, and the statistical data maintained
rating an agency's performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
SEE item number 1.1.067 for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_description:
Instructional materials developed by an
agency for training entities or individuals it
regulates or serves.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies as
required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed with
the Texas Ethics Commission.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Information resources and operational
strategic plans prepared in accordance
with 2054.095 and 2056.002, Government
Code.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Self evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans With Disabilities Act.

Records of temporary usefulness that are
not an integral part of a records series of
an agency, that are not regularly filed
within an agency’s recordkeeping system,
and that are required only for a limited
period of time for the completion of an
action by an official or employee of the
agency or in the preparation of an ongoing
records series. Transitory records are not
essential to the fulfillment of statutory
obligations or to the documentation of
agency functions. Some examples of
transitory information, which can be in any
medium (voice mail, fax, email, hard copy,
etc.) are routine messages; telephone
message notifications; internal meeting
notices; routing slips; incoming letters or
memoranda of transmittal that add
nothing of substance to enclosures; and
similar routine information used for
communication, but not for the
documentation, of a specific agency
transaction.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should use caution in
assigning this records series item
number to records of an agency to
make certain they are not part of
another records series listed in this
schedule or, for records series
unique to an agency, are not part of
a records series that documents the
fulfillment of the statutory
obligations of the agency or the
documentation of its functions. The
disposal of transitory information
need not be documented through
destruction signoffs (1.2.001) or in
records disposition logs (1.2.010),
but agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
those records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_description:
Official agenda and minutes of state
boards, committees, commissions, and
councils that conduct open meetings as
required by Government Code, Chapter
551.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met by
sending a copy to the Archives and
Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives
Commission. CAUTION: This
records series and item numbers
1.1.059, 1.1.060, 1.1.061, and
1.1.062 must be used for those
state boards, committees,
commissions, and councils, which
by law or the biennial
Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency.

series_title:
Meetings, Certified
Agendas or Tape
Recordings of Closed
series_description:
Certified agendas or tape recordings of
closed meetings of state boards,
commissions, committees, and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils from which written minutes are
prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils including exhibit items,
documentation for agenda items, etc.
Includes documents sent in advance of
meetings for briefing purposes, some of
which may not be submitted at an actual
meeting.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency
staff meetings.

Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating final
report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to
workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, and
similar activities.
comments:
CAUTION: SEE item number
1.1.064.

series_title:
Agency Rules, Policies, and
Procedures - Final
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating rules,
policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of the Attorney
General on an agency's Public Information
Act activities.

Transcripts and final decisions of hearings
conducted as part of the regulatory
process, and hearings on proposed rules
and changes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTES: Only records that
lead to substantial or statutory
changes to the operations of the
agency and its policies and
procedures should be retained for
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.
CAUTION: These records may be
maintained with related
information, including meeting
notices, proofs of publication, and
meeting minutes, and require a
longer retention period.

series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self-study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
self-evaluation report, agency
response to the commission's draft
report, and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations.

series_description:
Final agreement described by Government
Code 2009.054(c), associated with a
matter conducted under an alternative
dispute resolution procedure in which
personnel of a state agency participated as
a party on the agency's behalf.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies Code ,
Chapter 154.071.

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used internally
and externally by the agency, including
subsequent revisions to an agency form or
any associated design or design
modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR
105) that identifies the records that are
created and maintained by an agency,
provides the minimum timeframes the
records must be retained, and includes
instructions for their disposition. This
series may include working files and
documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Original is retained by the State and
Local Records Management
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description:
Agency copy (includes RMD 101, Tx-R-5,
306-58-1, and Agency Storage Forms).
Forms indicate records transferred to
storage or a transfer of legal custody.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
destroyed or transferred to the Archives
and Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives Commission,
showing records series title, dates of
records, and date destroyed or transferred.

Worksheets or working papers used to
capture records inventory information,
including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of a
records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Includes indexes, card files, shelf lists,
registers, guides, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Also includes documentation for disaster
recovery services provided by other
entities.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
Forms used by TSLAC (e.g. form RMD 113)
or other entities to establish disaster
recovery services, authorize agency staff to
access the media, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets of
complete and accurate electronic records.
Examples include, but are not limited to,
data tables of relational databases used by
applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of AC
to agency automated processing
files that the files do not fall under
records series listed elsewhere.

series_description:
Automated software applications and
operating system files including job control
language, program listing/source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, and equipment
control systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing of electronic records and their
timely, authorized disposition including
documentation describing how a system
operates and necessary for using the
system such as user guides, system or sub-
system definitions, system specifications,
input and output specifications, and
system flow charts; program descriptions
and documentation such as program
flowcharts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, and other records that
document modifications to computer
programs; and data documentation
necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated system
such as a data element dictionary, file
layout, code book or table, and other
records that explain the meaning, purpose,
structure, logical relationships, and origin
of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and Software
needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the
period of time required to access
the records.

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits
such as files or reports showing
transactions accepted, rejected,
suspended, and/or processed; history
files/tapes; records of online updates to
application files or security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Chargeback Records to
Data Processing Services
Users
series_description:
Records used to document, calculate costs,
and bill program units for computer usage
and data processing services. These
records are also used for cost recovery,
budgeting, or administrative purposes.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents
establishing data processing policies and
procedures in an agency in such areas as
access and security, systems development,
data retention and disposition, data
ownership, production control, system
back-up, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading or
processing of electronic records.
SEE item number 2.1.009.

series_description:
Forms and logs used to reconcile batches
submitted for processing against batches
received and processed.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Data resident on hard drives that make use
of user-specific information transmitted by
the Web server onto the user's computer
so that the information might be available
for later access by itself or other servers.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
The disposal of Internet cookies
need not be documented through
destruction authorizations (1.2.001)
or in records disposition logs
(1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part of
its records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to
activate the software for legal use by the
end users of a state agency, along with
warranties providing that the software will
perform in accordance with functional
specifications.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents whose
submission by candidates for vacant
positions is required on the application
form, by application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work-
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisorial level except in those agencies
with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection by employees of life,
disability, health, and other types of
insurance offered by the State of Texas to
its employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for item
number 3.2.001.

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

a) Executed, renewed, or amended on or
after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
b) Executed, renewed, or amended on or
before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description:
Records relating to the review of employee
grievances against personnel policies,
working conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the
U.S. Department of Labor. SEE item
number 1.1.048.

series_description:
Job evaluations, performance appraisals,
or other similar documents used to
evaluate the performance of an employee.

Corrective actions are those actions which
do not affect pay, status, or tenure and are
imposed to correct or improve an
employee's job performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: If, during the retention
period of this records series, any
part of this series is used to
document and support personnel
disciplinary action under item
number 3.1.021, all documentation
from this series used to support
disciplinary action must be retained
for the minimum retention period
described by item number 3.1.021.

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to the
best interests of the state, the agency, or
the employee work force; and for failure to
improve performance or conduct following
imposition of corrective action. May
include cause for demotion, suspension, or
dismissal, and the reasons for failure to
give written notice of resignation. May also
be used to document evidence of
employee self- improvement efforts, as
well as favorable and unfavorable
communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the records
of an employee including pay grade,
position classification, employee number,
evaluation date, and termination of
employment.

Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, transcripts, test
scores, or similar records documenting the
training, testing, or continuing education
achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Federal reporting form (INS I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation
requires that INS 1-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from date of
hire or 1 year after separation of
the employee, whichever later.
Agencies should make certain that
the INS I-9 forms for employees
who terminate from an agency less
than 3 years from date of hire are
kept for the 3 year retention period.

series_description:
Retention period applies if an agency
replies to the sender of a resume that it
will be kept on file should future job
openings occur.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
SEE item number 3.1.014 for
resumes, whether solicited or
unsolicited, that are used in any
way in the employment selection
process.

series_description:
Summary of applicant qualifications,
evaluation basis for selection or rejection,
original applications, job assignments,
promotions, separations, layoffs,
terminations, compensation records,
conditions of work, time records, hours of
training provided (separate from labor),
minority status, and gender of all selected
and rejected applicants.
Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Form completed and signed by employee
or official, or former employee or official,
electing to keep home address, home
telephone number, social security number,
and family information open or
confidential under the Public Information
Act, Government Code §552.024.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments: SEE item number 3.3.011.

series_description:
Consultation records, notes, letters,
memos, emails, reports and other
documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaint filed with EEO. If matter
becomes a grievance or the subject
of counseling or litigation, or
employee is subject to disciplinary
action, the records are subject to
retention in the appropriate records
series. SEE 1.1.048, 3.1.018,
3.1.020, and 3.1.021.3.1.006,

series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required deductions
from payroll, including garnishment or
other court-ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Payroll records and registers documenting
employee earnings, wages, and pay. This
may include but is not limited to payroll
input records, summary statements,
payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and
payroll history.

Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or
in the computation of taxes.

Employer's copy of "Employees'
Withholding Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Records defining the wage or salary rate
for each position in the agency expressed
in dollars, grades, or step numbers.

Records and documentation relating to
unemployment compensation claims,
including reimbursement of funds
disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission
(TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms used to deposit employee’s
earnings into a specified personal account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Records documenting the amount of
pension or deferred compensation earned
by individual employees.

Affirmative action plans for both regular
employees and apprenticeship programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Reports providing statistical information
on labor force.

Minimum information needed to verify
employment, includes name, social
security number, exact dates of
employment, last known address and most
recent public access option form.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years
comments: See item number 3.1.038.

series_description:
Records relating to review and monitoring
of job classifications within an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules,
rosters, or assignments.

Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.

series_title:
Reimbursable Activities,
Requests and
Authorizations to Engage
in
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define
agency wide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any documents detailing the procedural
duties and responsibilities of agency
positions on a position-by-position basis.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 4 years

series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name,
staff hired during a month, detailed listings
of employees within its organizational
structure, position vacancies, analyses of
turnover rates and seasonality of
employment, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Aptitude or skills tests required of job
applicants or of current personnel to
qualify for promotion or transfer.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different test (different in terms of
either questions or administration
procedures) should be retained for
the period indicated.

series_description:
Aptitude and skills test papers of job
applicants or of current personnel taking a
test to qualify for promotion or transfer.

Includes documentation used to complete
EEO reports.

Reports, studies, aggregated or summary
data, and similar documentation compiled
to monitor and demonstrate compliance
with the federal Equal Pay Act

Used to create employee leave balances,
to transfer leave balances when an
employee transfers positions, to adjust
carry-over balances at end of fiscal year, to
correct errors on leave accumulation, and
to close out leave accounts on separated
employees.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle
and provides employee leave status
information for each position.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting individual
employee’s hours worked, including work
schedules and documentation evidencing
adherence to or deviation from normal
hours for those employees working on
fixed schedules.

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick Leave
Requests
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for vacation,
compensatory, sick, Family and Medical
Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and
other types of authorized leave, and
supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Requests submitted, approvals, number of
hours transferred in and out, etc.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Claims; invoices; statements; copies of
checks and purchase orders; expenditure
authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance,
purchase, general journal, expenditure,
and special vouchers; and similar records
that serve to document disbursements,
including those documenting claims for
and reimbursement to employees for
travel and other expenses.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants,
cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts
books, cash transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Periodic reports compiled from
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Yearly report compiled from information
entered into the Uniform Statewide
Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting the internal and
external procedural requirements with
respect to the accounting department of a
state agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Records of accounts deemed uncollectible,
including write-off authorizations and
returned checks, warrants, and drafts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Documentation relating to the capital and
fixed assets of a state agency, including
equipment or property history cards
containing data on initial cost,
depreciation schedules or summaries used
for capital outlay budgeting, and property
sale, auction, or disposal records of agency
owned equipment and property.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting requests for
payment of a sum of money according to
the terms of a policy or contract.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This series documents state, federal, and
other sponsored grant projects
participated in or administered by state
agencies. It includes grant authorization
records, which provide evidence of the
award of grants to or by agencies;
grant/project financial or performance
reports, which are periodic reports of
financial activity and/or program
performance related to grants received or
made by agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Retention requirements
may vary depending on the specific
funding agency. Records in this
series may be subject to
Government Code 441.1855.
Agencies must ensure that records
are retained for the appropriate
retention period.

series_description:
Non-awarded, denied, or unfunded grant
applications and proposals.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Records documenting financial obligations
of a state agency that are not payable
within one year of the date of the balance
sheet, including debentures, loans,
deferred tax liabilities, bonds, and pension
obligations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Signature cards or similar records
establishing authority of an agency
employee to initiate or authorize financial
transactions on behalf of an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Bank statements, credit card statements,
cancelled checks, check registers, deposit
slips, debit and credit notices,
reconciliations, and other banking related
records.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Required by the General Appropriations
Act. Includes encumbrances and
documentation about budget transfers and
revisions, as well as detail charts of
accounts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting incoming or
outgoing deliveries, including through
private courier services.

Any mailing address, telephone or fax
number, or email address records
maintained by an agency on its employees
or on entities or persons it serves.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Records and reports of postage expenses,
including postal meter usage.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_title:
Requisitions for In-Agency
or Inter-Agency
Copy/Printing Service
series_description:
Includes word processing and data
processing.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Licenses and permits obtained from
external agencies or organizations in order
to perform operations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include licenses
and permits issued by an agency as
part of its statutory responsibilities.

series_title:
Photocopier and Telefax
Usage Logs and Reports
series_description:
Registers or logs of print copies and fax
transmissions made by user or in total.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Schedules of prices charged by an agency
for services to the public or other agencies,
including any documentation used to
determine the charges.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Liability, theft, fire, health, life,
automobile, and other policies for
government property and personnel
including supporting documentation
relevant to the implementation,
modification, renewal, or replacement of
policies. Executed, renewed, or amended
on or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Liability, theft, fire, health, life,
automobile, and other policies for
government property and personnel
including supporting documentation
relevant to the implementation,
modification, renewal, or replacement of
policies. Executed, renewed, or amended
on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description:
Any internally distributed manual,
guidelines, or similar records that establish
standard office procedures for an agency,
e.g. agency style manuals, telephone
protocols, mail room procedures, print
shop and photocopy ordering instructions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any record created by an agency to track
any type of incoming and outgoing
correspondence or packages by the U.S.
Postal Service (USPS) or by private
couriers.

List of agency contracts, leases, and
agreements including general obligation,
land lease, utilities, and construction
contracts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Assessments or evaluations of the value of
state-owned buildings or property.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Records documenting building space
requests and approvals. Information on
request forms may include but is not
limited to: the building and room; the
reason for the change; parties involved in
the request; date of request and approval.

Records documenting the determination,
checking, or rectifying of any instrument
giving quantitative measurements.
comments:
CAUTION: Some equipment and
instruments may require longer
retention period. State agencies
must determine if longer retention
period is required based on the
type of equipment or instruments
they use within their agency.

retention_statement: Life of Asset
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title:
Estimate Files (Supply and
Repair Cost Estimates)
series_description:
Quotes for minor construction and repair
projects and supplies. Includes supporting
documentation, as applicable.

Requests or work orders for repairs or
maintenance to facilities, vehicles, or
equipment completed by internal agency
personnel or external service providers.

Any type of usage report or log used to
monitor utilities such as gas, electric,
water, etc.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include utility
usage reports for agencies that
operate their own utilities.

series_description:
Equipment and material descriptions and
specifications that may include but are not
limited to detailed descriptions; lists of raw
materials and ingredients; physical
characteristics of items; special handling
procedures; or technical drawings.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Reservation logs or similar records relating
to the use of agency facilities such as
meeting rooms, auditoriums, etc.

Reports summarizing efficiency of facility
space utilization, which may include data
on room usage, demand, allocation, and
capacity.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Documentation of mail returned by the
postal or other mail delivery services as
undeliverable.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: If corrected address
provided and mail rerouted,
maintain address update
documentation for prescribed
retention period.

series_description:
Freight or cargo claims against carrier for
damage or loss to a shipment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Shipping information, including order
acknowledgements, packing slips, and
related documentation.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Some shipping
information may require longer
retention period. State agencies
must determine if longer retention
period is required based on the
type of materials shipped to or from
their agency.

series_description:
Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, awarded and unawarded
bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations.
Associated with a contract executed,
renewed, or amended on or after
September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, awarded and unawarded
bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations.
Associated with a contract executed,
renewed, or amended on or before August
31, 2015.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description:
Invalid bids that do not meet agency
submission requirements and are not
included in bid evaluation process (e.g.
withdrawn, missed submission deadline,
incomplete submission, etc.)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Log, register, etc., providing a record of
purchase orders issued, orders received,
and similar data on procurement status.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
W-9 IRS Form used to request a taxpayer
identification number (TIN) for reporting
information to the Internal Revenue
Service (IRS). This includes W-9 forms
received by a state agency from vendors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Plans for evacuation of agency facilities in
cases of emergency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Plan that provides information to
employees about the hazardous chemicals
to which they may be exposed to in their
workplace.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 5 years

series_description:
List of each hazardous chemical normally
present in the workplace.

Logs, registers, or similar records
documenting visitors to limited access or
restricted areas of agency facilities.

Records relating to the issuance of keys,
identification cards, building passes,
passwords, signed statements or similar
instruments of access to agency or state
facilities, equipment or automated
systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Documentation relating to disputed long
distance calls, including documents
evidencing repayment by employees for
personal long distance use.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Logs and related documentation used to
maintain information about state owned
aircraft flight data.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Logs and related documentation used to
maintain information about leased aircraft
flight data.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: List of passengers on an aircraft.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Includes mileage, fuel/oil consumption,
passengers carried and other related
operational information.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Vehicle titles, registration information, and
owner manuals for state vehicles.
retention_statement: Life of Asset

series_description:
All documentation to establish deny,
modify, continue or end membership as a
Participating Department in the TESRS
Pension System, except those listed
elsewhere on this schedule. Incudes
records such as but not limited to:
applications, approvals, authorizations,
agreements, calculations, cancellations,
contracts, civic records and documents,
court or legal documents, designations,
correspondence, estimates, ledgers, notes,
modifications, opinions and advice,
receipts, reports, reviews, selections,
statements, terminations, and worksheets.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
Remarks: Within 90 days of receipt
or production, documentation in
paper format is scanned into the
agency’s document management
system. The electronic
documentation becomes the official
documentation with the value of
original and paper source
documentation is destroyed.

series_description:
All documentation to establish, deny,
modify, continue or end membership in
the TESRS Pension System, except those
listed elsewhere on this schedule. Incudes
records such as but not limited to:
affidavits, appeals, applications, approvals,
authorizations, agreements, calculations,
cancellations, certificates, complaints,
contracts, court or legal documents,
designations, correspondence, estimates,
ledgers, medical records, military service
records, notes, modifications, opinions and
advice, receipts, reports, requests, reviews,
selections, statements, terminations,
verifications, vouchers, waivers, and
worksheets.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 30 years
comments:
Remarks: Within 90 days of receipt
or production, documentation in
paper format is scanned into the
agency’s document management
system. The electronic
documentation becomes the official
documentation with the value of
original and paper source
documentation is destroyed.

series_description:
All documentation to establish, deny,
modify, continue or end benefits provided
under TESRS, except those listed
elsewhere on this schedule. Incudes
records such as but not limited to:
affidavits, appeals, applications, approvals,
authorizations, agreements, calculations,
cancellations, certificates, complaints,
contracts, court or legal documents,
designations, correspondence, estimates,
ledgers, medical records, military service
records, notes, modifications, opinions and
advice, receipts, reports, requests, reviews,
selections, statements, terminations,
verifications, vouchers, waivers, and
worksheets.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 30 years
comments:
Remarks: Within 90 days of receipt
or production, documentation in
paper format is scanned into the
agency’s document management
system. The electronic
documentation becomes the official
documentation with the value of
original and paper source
documentation is destroyed.
